verdict.answer_first: B; sentence 1 gives the bottom line directly: "image is a static template; a container is a running instance" while A spends half a sentence on "related but distinct concepts" first.
verdict.decisiveness: B; it keeps one controlling model, "static template" vs "running instance," while A stacks several frames: "blueprint," "recipe / class blueprint," "cooked meal / built house."
verdict.actionability: B; the concrete test is more reproducible: "docker pull ubuntu" then "docker run ubuntu" three times gives "three independent containers"; A gives useful commands but less outcome proof.
